market_overview.headline: Risk-on momentum continues with SPY and QQQ both in Full Bull structure, though recent EMA spreads are narrow suggesting consolidation.
market_overview.detail: 41% of stocks in Full Bull setup vs 19% Full Bear, with tech hardware leading momentum. Both major indices showing bearish cross risk on tight EMA spreads, while BTC remains in Bear Rally structure.

reversal_candidates.headline: Bear Rally Reversal Candidates (Bullish Crossover Potential)

items.name: Synopsys, Inc.
items.note: EDA software leader with 8W EMA just 0.02% below 13W, showing strong reversal momentum in beaten-down tech services.

items.name: JP Morgan Chase & Co.
items.crossover_detail: 8W just below 13W (0.04%) — bullish cross potential; 13W just below 21W (0.12%) — bullish cross potential
items.note: Mega-cap bank with dual bullish crossover potential as EMAs compress near reversal points.

items.note: Cybersecurity leader rallying 7.8% above 8W with bullish crossover setup developing.

items.note: Asset management giant showing bear rally strength with 8W approaching bullish cross above 13W.

items.note: EDA software stock with 8W just 0.01% below 13W, primed for imminent bullish cross.

items.note: Low volume pullback in energy leader suggests orderly selling, quality entry opportunity.

items.name: Cheniere Energy, Inc.
items.note: LNG export leader with low volume pullback to 13W support, maintaining 6% cushion above 21W.

items.name: Freeport-McMoRan, Inc.
items.note: Copper play pulling back on normal volume, holding well above 21W EMA in established uptrend.

items.name: Howmet Aerospace Inc.
items.note: Aerospace supplier with low volume pullback suggests institutional accumulation rather than distribution.

momentum_themes.headline: Tech Hardware Acceleration
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology stocks dominating momentum with storage and semiconductor names leading. Data center buildout and AI infrastructure themes driving accelerated buying in hardware plays.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Leading, Tech Services Lagging

strongest.note: Perfect bullish setup with all 14 names in bullish structure, benefiting from Iran tensions.
Industrial momentum continues with 94% bullish participation.
Hardware acceleration driving 77% bullish participation despite some pullbacks.

weakest.note: Software stocks struggling with 72% bearish participation as rotation favors hardware.
Biotech weakness persists with 59% bearish participation.
Banks mixed despite some reversal candidates emerging from bear structure.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from software to hardware as AI infrastructure spending accelerates. Energy benefiting from geopolitical tensions while healthcare remains under pressure.

items.text: SPY and QQQ both showing bearish cross risk with tight EMA spreads — watch for consolidation
CNQ high volume pullback (3.0x avg) suggests institutional selling pressure
57 stocks in Full Bear structure including mega-caps TSLA, LLY, MSFT
BTC lagging major indices in Bear Rally structure could signal crypto weakness

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iran geopolitical tensions dominated headlines with Trump signaling openness to negotiations while naval tensions persist. Cramer warned about AI stock concentration risks as earnings season approaches. Hardware vs software split reflects ongoing sector rotation themes.

items.text: Iran-US diplomatic developments creating energy sector volatility
AI infrastructure spending narrative driving hardware outperformance
Earnings season setup creating pre-announcement positioning
Geopolitical risk premium supporting defense and energy names
